discrepancies.title: Q1 PROVISIONAL qualifier records EXCERPT-2-only access to 109-EXEC handoff
discrepancies.description: Synth had access only to EXCERPT 2's verdict-summary in prompt 112 §1.5, not the full 22909fe handoff. For cascade-control purposes the closure is operationally sufficient, but full FW-source verification (i.e. inspecting whether 109-EXEC's NO_GO transcends specific FW Prop 4.1 pull-back to all FW-anchored Route B variants) would require independent reading of 22909fe. Priority is low because no specific FW non-pull-back variant has been named in any prior round.
discrepancies.follow_up: OPTIONAL_LOW_PRIORITY: independent reading of 22909fe handoff for foundational closure of all FW-anchored Route B variants (a hypothetical Conte-Musette extension or different FW §4 Hamiltonian shift would be a NEW sub-mechanism requiring its own dispatch, not a re-evaluation of Prop 4.1)

discrepancies.title: Q2 partial-retention infrastructure-vs-evidence distinction
discrepancies.description: Synth notes a small partial-retention possibility at the INFRASTRUCTURE level (reusable Lax-pair numerical solver code from 110-EXEC; V_quad-side numerical-stability telemetry that does not pass through jm_ueno_inversion_via_fw and would not inherit the IDENTITY_ON_CITED tautology). At the PROBATIVE-EVIDENCE level (the level Q2 asks about), demotion is total. EXCERPT 4 of prompt 112 did not specifically address infrastructure preservation in 110's deliverables.
discrepancies.synth_confidence: HIGH at probative level; SMALL_OPEN at infrastructure level
discrepancies.follow_up: OPTIONAL_RECORD_DISTINCTION: SQL-todo annotation noting that future Route D re-attempts (with a true inversion implementation, not identity-on-cited) can reuse 110-EXEC infrastructure components. This is a SQL-todo level concern, not a cascade-level concern.

discrepancies.title: Q3 residual KNY §8.5 surrounding-sections scan possibility (low priority)
discrepancies.description: Synth notes a residual possibility that an UN-NORMALIZED KNY Hamiltonian display in §8.5 surrounding sections might in principle revive Route A. However synth concludes this combination is unlikely given off-generic-stratum diagnosis would force the same structural failure for any generic-machinery-of-any-form targeting V_quad's degenerate-locus point. Flagged as LOW-PRIORITY thoroughness check.
discrepancies.synth_confidence: HIGH on Route A closure for forward-cascade purposes
discrepancies.follow_up: OPTIONAL_LOW_PRIORITY: KNY §8.5 surrounding-sections scan (~30 min) for thoroughness; unlikely to revive Route A but documents that the synth's NO_GO is robust against this residual possibility

discrepancies.title: Q6 cascade-table TIER alignment edge case (Q4_HEDGE × Q3_NO_GO)
discrepancies.description: §6 cascade table (in prompt 112 §6 B1-rewritten form) flags Q4 = HEDGE × Q3 = NO_GO as an edge case requiring synth clarification. Synth provided the clarification: the HEDGE is between Route F (substrate-paste-pending) and downstream fallback (Path-delta / governance), NOT between Route F and Route A. Drafting Route A in parallel with Route F would be wasteful given Route A's NO_GO. The parallel structure should be Route F (analytic) + governance amendment (safety valve), not Route F + Route A.
discrepancies.synth_confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH (edge case resolved by synth's explicit clarification)
discrepancies.follow_up: GOVERNANCE_NOTE: prompt 112 §6 cascade-table B1-rewrite anticipates this clarification path correctly; the TIER 2 row's 'request synth clarification' branch was actively used by synth and produced a clean result. No §6 amendment needed for next prompt-drafting cycle.

discrepancies.title: GOVERNANCE_PARALLEL recommendation is judgment, not analytic necessity
discrepancies.description: Synth caveat 4 of Q8: GOVERNANCE_PARALLEL recommendation is a judgment call. Case for it: project has been gated on R1 closure across 097 → 069r1 → 069r2 → 069r3 (4 cycles); pre-staging amendment costs little and provides deployable fallback within the Route F substrate-paste latency. Case against: pre-staging fallbacks during an active investigation can signal lack of conviction in the primary path, and may be premature given Route F has not yet been substrate-tested. Synth leans Y based on cycle-count / project-blocked considerations, but operator's judgment may differ.
discrepancies.follow_up: OPERATOR_DECISION: accept synth's GOVERNANCE_PARALLEL=Y recommendation for parallel-staging or override with Route-F-first-only ordering. If accepted: queue umbrella v2.x amendment drafting prompt in parallel with §10 substrate paste.
